distance_km.note: Multiple sources confirm the main 63 km distance from Otepää to Elva

elevation_m.note: No web sources in search results provide specific elevation gain data. Sources describe the course as 'constantly rolls over the hills' but do not quantify total elevation gain

country.note: Multiple sources confirm Estonia as the country

region_state_province.note: Sources mention Otepää Upland and Elva as locations, and one source references 'Valga maakond' (Valga County), but Tartu County is not explicitly confirmed in search results

discipline.note: Sources confirm both classic and freestyle techniques are offered. The 63 km main race is classic only, but 31 km freestyle race is also available

typical_participants.note: Sources cite 'up to 5,000 skiers' for 2026 and 'usually there are 6,000 skiers' historically. The claim of ~4,000 starters is within reasonable tolerance

year_founded.note: Multiple sources confirm the race was first held in 1960, not 1966. Wikipedia states 'It debuted in 1960' and sources reference '65 years' of history from 1960

official_website.note: The URL https://tartumaraton.ee is confirmed in multiple search results as the official website

typical_race_date.note: Wikipedia and multiple sources confirm the race is held 'on the third Sunday of February.' The 2026 edition is scheduled for February 14-15, which aligns with mid-February timing
